clusters.title: Explosives discovered near gas pipeline to Hungary, says Serbia's president
clusters.neutral_headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election

Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: Explosives discovered near gas pipeline to Hungary, says Serbia's president
items.standfirst: Powerful explosives were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline in Serbia that carries Russian gas to Hungary, the leaders of the two countries said on Sunday, triggering political scrutiny in Hungary days before a national election.
items.content: Powerful explosives were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline in Serbia that carries Russian gas to Hungary, the leaders of the two countries said on Sunday, triggering political scrutiny in Hungary days before a national election.

items.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
items.standfirst: The incident comes a week before the polls, and follow warnings of a potential operations staged to influence voters.
items.content: The incident comes a week before the polls, and follow warnings of a potential operations staged to influence voters.

items.title: Hungarian PM faces ‘false flag’ claims after Serbia says explosives found near pipeline
items.standfirst: Incident prompts political scrutiny across Hungary as Viktor Orbán trails in polls before next Sunday’s electionSerbia has said it found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond, sparking claims by Hungary’s leading opposition candidate of a possible “false flag” operation aimed at influencing the country’s elections.On Sunday, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, said he had been informed by Serbia’s president, Aleksandar Vučić, of the discovery near an extension of the TurkStream pipeline, which transports Russian gas through the Balkans to central and eastern Europe. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
clusters.neutral_headline: Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
bullet_summary: Dozens of people are missing and at least two confirmed dead after a boat carrying migrants capsized off coast of Libya
At least 70 people were missing after a migrant boat, which left the Libyan coast, capsized in the Mediterranean, an Italian NGO said Sunday
Two people died and 32 were rescued from the vessels, said the NGO

clusters.title: Israel launches new strikes on Lebanon, forces closure of Syria border crossing

items.title: Israel launches new strikes on Lebanon, forces closure of Syria border crossing
items.standfirst: Israeli airstrikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon on Sunday killed at least 11 people, including a family of six, and wounded dozens more. The attacks also prompted Syria to temporarily close its main border crossing with Lebanon, as Israel's conflict with Hezbollah intensifies.
items.content: Israeli airstrikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon on Sunday killed at least 11 people, including a family of six, and wounded dozens more. The attacks also prompted Syria to temporarily close its main border crossing with Lebanon, as Israel's conflict with Hezbollah intensifies.

items.title: Fugitive mafia boss wanted for murder arrested at Amalfi coast luxury villa
items.standfirst: Roberto Mazzarella, head of a notorious Camorra clan, had been on the run for more than a yearAn Italian mafia boss, who was one of Italy’s most dangerous fugitives, has been arrested on murder charges after more than a year on the run, Italian police said on Saturday.Roberto Mazzarella was the head of the notorious Mazzarella clan of the Camorra – the Naples-based organised crime gang. Continue reading...
